intro:
I couldn't find information on 'Limniris pseudacorus'. However, I found 'Limniris' to be an incorrect common name. I did find 'Limniris' to be a synonym for 'Iris pseudacorus'. Here's a paragraph for 'Iris pseudacorus':

Iris pseudacorus, commonly known as yellow flag, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe. The health benefits of Iris pseudacorus include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Iris pseudacorus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Iris pseudacorus has been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being used in ointments and creams for skin issues and wound healing.
